title: campanula incanescens var. kermanica
intro: Campanula incanescens var. kermanica, also known as Kerman bellflower, is a variety of flowering plant in the Campanula genus, native to the mountainous regions of Iran. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Kerman bellflower include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of Campanula incanescens var. kermanica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
